title: SuperBEST v4 Full Structural Audit

entries.lower_bound: 0-node impossible: no terminal in {x} ∪ Q equals e^x
0-node impossible: terminal x gives identity, constants are fixed
T09: no single F16-operator has constant derivative -1 for all x

entries.upper_bound: EML(EXL(0,x)/2, 1) = exp((ln x)/2) = sqrt(x) in 2 nodes
entries.lower_bound: Derivative obstruction: all F16 operators O(x,c) have derivative involving e^(±x) or constants, not (1/2)x^(-1/2); all O(c,x) fail via singularity or wrong power-function exponent under rational-terminal convention
entries.gap_if_any: Convention gap: EPL(-ln(2), x) = x^(1/2) is a 1-node construction under real-constant convention. Under rational-terminal convention (Q terminals only), -ln(2) is inadmissible as a leaf, so 2-node lower bound holds. T08 uses rational-terminal convention.

entries.upper_bound: LEdiv(x, EML(y, 1)) = x - ln(e^y) = x - y in 2 nodes
entries.lower_bound: T33 structural: no O in F16 achieves ∂O/∂x=+1 AND ∂O/∂y=-1 simultaneously; LEdiv is closest (∂x=1 but ∂y=-1/y≠-1)

entries.upper_bound: EML(LEAd(EXL(0,x), y), 1) = exp(ln(x+y)) = x+y in 3 nodes
entries.lower_bound: T08-A: ∂O/∂y ≠ 1 for all O in F16 (1-node); no 2-node tree achieves ∂T/∂y≡1 (2-node)

entries.upper_bound: ELAd(EXL(0,x), y) = e^(ln x) * y = xy in 2 nodes
entries.lower_bound: T32: derivative obstruction shows no single F16-node computes xy

entries.upper_bound: EML(EXL(ln(n), x), 1) = exp(n*ln(x)) = x^n in 2-3 nodes (3 under raw-integer-n convention)
entries.lower_bound: T08-P: x^n = exp(n*ln(x)) requires 3 distinct intermediates (ln x, n*ln x, exp) under raw-integer-n convention; derivative obstruction rules out 1-node
entries.gap_if_any: Convention gap: EPL(n, x) = (e^n)^(ln x) = x^n is 1-node if integer n is a compiled constant. Under raw-integer convention (n must be computed from unit constants), 3 nodes required. T08 uses raw-integer convention.

entries.upper_bound: ELSb(EXL(0,x), y) = e^(ln x)/y = x/y in 2 nodes (not 1)
entries.lower_bound: Structural: no O in F16 computes x/y in 1 node. ELSb(x,y)=e^x/y requires e^x=x (impossible on (0,∞)); all Class-I operators have wrong derivative structure; Class-II operators similarly fail.
entries.gap_if_any: TABLE ERROR: v4 records div=1 node but structural proof shows SB(div)≥2. The 1-node claim appears to conflate recip(x)=1/x (ELSb(0,x), genuinely 1 node) with div(x,y)=x/y (requires 2 nodes). Correct the table to 2 nodes. Total SuperBEST corrected: 19 nodes.
entries.prior_source: R16_T08_Structural_Proofs.tex Remark 5.2 (div requires 2 nodes via ELSb)

t08_verdict_explanation: T08 cannot be upgraded to THEOREM as stated because the div entry contains a genuine table error (records 1 node, correct value is 2 nodes). Under the corrected 19-node table, all structural proofs are in place and T08 achieves THEOREM status.
all_proved_explanation: 9 of 10 entries are proved (THEOREM-level structural proofs). The div entry is NOT proved at 1 node — it is an inconsistency. The corrected div=2 entry IS proved.

new_proofs_in_this_audit.convention_gap: EPL(-ln2, x)=sqrt(x) is 1-node under real-constant convention

convention_registry.description: Leaf constants drawn from Q (rationals). Transcendental values like ln(2) or ln(n) require their own nodes.
Integer exponent n is a raw terminal; ln(n) must be computed as a separate node.
All 16 operators including ELSb with raw second argument.

recommendation: Correct the SuperBEST v4 table: change div from 1 node to 2 nodes. Total: 19 nodes. Then all 10 entries have THEOREM-level structural proofs and T08 can be fully upgraded to THEOREM status.
